title: The Perfect Fusion of AI and Human Expertise: MoneySkills Launches the Ultimate Hybrid Cryptocurrency Trading Bot for 2026
description: Overcome the limitations of AI through real-time expert optimization to achieve fully automated, high-yield trading. New York, NY; Los Angeles, CA, May 09, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- As artificial intelligence rapidly permeates the realm of cryptocurrency trading, AI agents are being hailed as the next wave of financial innovation. Driving this transformation is MoneySkills, which today announced the launch of its groundbreaking, free AI trading bot. By combining powerful AI computing capabilities with real-time strategic optimization from a team of financial experts, MoneySkills has created a seamless, efficient platform that stands as the premier AI cryptocurrency trading solution of 2026. The Rise of Artificial Intelligence in Cryptocurrency Trading: A New Era of Automation As artificial intelligence technology continues to evolve, it is fundamentally revolutionizing the way digital assets are traded. Leveraging advanced algorithms, traders can now automate complex trading strategies, optimize decision-making, and execute trades at lightning speed. This automation eliminates human emotion and hesitation, thereby enabling a purely data-driven approach. Today, AI not only enhances the effectiveness of trading strategies but also further democratizes the cryptocurrency market, making it accessible to everyone. Revolutionary Hybrid Mode: Fully Automated and Expert-Optimized. While artificial intelligence possesses astonishing speed and data processing capabilities, it sometimes lacks the nuanced predictive insight characteristic of experienced traders. MoneySkills’ AI trading bots are designed to bridge this gap. The platform operates in a fully automated manner-users need only complete the initial setup, after which the bots take full charge of market analysis and trade execution. What truly sets MoneySkills apart is its integration of artificial intelligence with real-time expert strategy optimization. AI ensures rapid and precise decision-making, while oversight by human experts guarantees that the system consistently maintains peak performance. Recent industry research indicates that platforms combining AI with human intervention significantly outperform those relying solely on either technology alone. MoneySkills' hybrid model validates this finding, representing the future trajectory of automated wealth creation. Industry Validation: The Increasingly Important Role of Artificial Intelligence in the Financial Sector Recent developments in the fintech sector underscore the transformative impact of artificial intelligence. Leading financial institutions like Revolut are actively exploring AI to construct scalable, energy-efficient cryptocurrency trading systems, while AI-centric crypto projects such as Bittensor are gaining significant momentum. The trend is clear: systems capable of processing massive datasets and adapting to market trends in real time are reshaping both trading tools and the underlying assets themselves. How to Start Automated Cryptocurrency Trading with MoneySkills? Getting started with MoneySkills is incredibly simple and requires no technical expertise. In just a few steps, anyone can deploy a powerful trading bot: 1. Register an Account: Visit the official MoneySkills website to create a free account. New users receive an exclusive welcome bonus: $15 in real trading funds + $50 in trial funds. 2. Set Your Strategy: You can choose from a range of preset trading strategies meticulously designed by experts (such as trend following or mean reversion), or customize your settings to align with your specific financial goals. 3. Launch the Bot: With just a single click, the MoneySkills AI trading bot begins analyzing real-time market data and executing optimized trades on your behalf. 4. Monitoring and Withdrawals: Track your automated earnings anytime, anywhere, and enjoy fast, secure withdrawals to your personal account. Why Choose MoneySkills for 2026? ⦁ Fully Automated Trading: AI handles all market analysis and execution, offering you a seamless and effortless experience. ⦁ Real-Time Expert Optimization: A perfect fusion of AI speed and human financial expertise ensures continuous strategy refinement. ⦁ Unparalleled Ease of Use: An intuitive interface makes it the ideal choice for both absolute beginners and seasoned veterans. ⦁ Robust Risk Management: Comprehensive built-in risk management tools safeguard your investments against market volatility. MoneySkills simplifies the cryptocurrency trading process while maintaining exceptional profitability, enabling users to navigate the highly volatile crypto market with confidence. About MoneySkills: MoneySkills is a leading cryptocurrency trading platform dedicated to automating the trading process and maximizing returns. Leveraging advanced AI technology and expert-optimized trading strategies, the platform provides fully automated, secure, and high-yield trading solutions for both novice and experienced investors. To claim your welcome bonus and immediately start automating the management of your cryptocurrency portfolio, please visit the official Money Skills website. title: Wall Street Keeps Testing AI Traders, But Most Are Still Underperforming
description: Wall Street Keeps Testing AI Traders, But Most Are Still Underperforming Recent trading competitions suggest large language models are still unreliable portfolio managers, according to Bloomberg . Tests involving models from OpenAI, Anthropic, Google, and xAI have often delivered underwhelming results: many lost money, traded excessively, and made erratic decisions despite receiving identical prompts. In several cases, models appeared unable to stick to coherent strategies for more than a few trading sessions. Bloomberg writes that one of the clearest examples came from Alpha Arena, a competition created by startup Nof1. Eight models were each given $10,000 and asked to trade U.S. tech stocks over a two-week period using different strategies, including defensive approaches and leveraged bets. Across four competitions, the models collectively lost roughly a third of their capital, and only six of 32 outcomes ended in profit. The gap in behavior was striking: xAI’s Grok 4.20 made just 158 trades in one contest, while Alibaba Group’s Qwen executed 1,418 under the exact same prompt. The experiments reflect growing interest in whether generative AI can eventually outperform traditional fund managers. Wall Street firms including JPMorgan Chase and Balyasny Asset Management already rely on AI for research, fraud detection, and internal analysis, but they have largely stopped short of handing over actual investment decisions. As Nof1 founder Jay Azhang put it, current models still struggle with basics like “position sizing, timing, signal weighting and overtrading.” That broader pattern has shown up elsewhere too. Research blog Flat Circle tracked 11 public AI trading competitions and found that while every event produced at least one profitable model, only two generated a profitable median return — suggesting most bots still underperform more often than not. Azhang was even more blunt about the state of autonomous trading: giving an LLM money and letting it invest independently “isn’t a thing yet.” Some firms are still betting that the technology improves with better tools and tighter guardrails. Intelligent Alpha, for example, runs an AI-driven fund that combines LLMs with earnings transcripts, analyst forecasts, corporate filings, macroeconomic indicators, and web searches to make predictions. In late 2025, OpenAI’s ChatGPT correctly predicted the direction of earnings estimate revisions 68% of the time — its strongest showing so far. Evaluating these systems remains difficult because traditional backtesting methods can be misleading: models may already have embedded knowledge of past market events, creating look-ahead bias. That has pushed more firms toward live-market experiments, where results so far suggest AI may be useful as an assistant — but not a replacement — for human traders. Tyler Durden Sat, 05/09/2026 - 15:45
